title: Sideroxylon euphlebium
taxonomy: Sideroxylon euphlebium is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. It is native to Australia and is considered an endangered species due to habitat loss and fragmentation. The classification of Sideroxylon euphlebium is within the genus Sideroxylon, which is a part of the Sapotaceae family. The plant is often referred to as a remnant of the ancient Gondwana supercontinent. This rare species is closely related to other Sapotaceae plants native to Australia.
